what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter metadata record that registers an attachment (att-e0c69d72) in the dragnet-development channel memory, marking it as type board_items and pointing it at board item item-a385f1ca with an added timestamp.
observations: Only YAML frontmatter is present; there is no body content.
The target is expressed as a board:// URI: board://item-a385f1ca.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme.
The file is purely a linkage/provenance record with no payload.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields (id, channel, attachment_type, target, added).
Uses an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp with explicit +00:00 offset.
Uses a URI-style reference for an external board item target.
concerns: The file body is empty, so the actual attachment content or payload is not present.
The board:// URI target cannot be resolved or verified from within this file alone.
There is no author, source message, or revision information.
notes: A bare channel-memory attachment record that links att-e0c69d72 to board item a385f1ca in dragnet-development, useful only as a traceability stub.
questions: What content or payload does attachment att-e0c69d72 represent?
What is board item a385f1ca and what is its relationship to the channel?
Was this attachment generated from a specific message, event, or user action?
apparent_themes: URI-based linking to external board items
intention_signals: It appears intended to capture and preserve the fact that a board item is attached to or referenced within the dragnet-development channel memory, creating a retrievable, timestamped link.
novelty: The target field uses a board:// URI scheme rather than a conventional file path or message identifier.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped provenance link between attachment att-e0c69d72 and board item item-a385f1ca in the dragnet-development channel, supporting traceability of board-item references within channel memory.
